title: Carduus songoricus
taxonomy: Carduus songoricus is classified as a species of thistle within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Carduus, which comprises several species of thistles. The species Carduus songoricus is native to China and is found in certain mountainous regions. The plant is known for its spiny stems and leaves, characteristic of many thistle species. Carduus songoricus is often found growing in rocky and dry areas.

intro: Carduus songoricus, also known as Mongolian thistle, is a type of thistle plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Carduus songoricus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Carduus songoricus is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used in traditional Mongolian medicine to support digestive health and alleviate various symptoms.
